items.title: /mu/ Music: Lily Allen: It's Not Me It's You [Capitol, 2009] She is too a role model--for ch…
items.content_text: Lily Allen: It's Not Me It's You [Capitol, 2009] She is too a role model--for chart queens, bohemians born-and-raised, and paparazzi victims everywhere. True, her synth-pop album debuted below India.Arie at five before its SoundScan swan dive. But even diving she's more graceful than most, and she has every expectation of popping out of the pool and climbing the ladder again, which is how her first one went gold. Here the modestly likable, oddly uncategorizable singer of Alright, Still emerges a

items.title: /o/ Auto: The Last of Old World Luxury
items.content_text: They don't make 'em like they used to... at least not for us proletariat in the entry white-collar class. This isn't an economic discussion. When you get into a new luxury car it feels like tin by comparison, my dad had a 2000 bmw 740iL. I don't claim to know a lot about that car, but when i sat in the backseat I felt like I was in absolute first class. Real wood, leather, that big cabin, instead of some slop tablet new world order eye-tracking camera smartphone-mobile. I bought a volvo xc90 bec

items.title: /v/ Video Games: SNES
items.content_text: Out of every single console in existence, this one is just the best. The controller is amazing. The games are amazing. The graphics are amazing. The sound is amazing. The only issue I have is that it doesn't play NES carts, but it can play gameboy carts with the super gameboy. But still, amazing system. Easily the best.

items.title: /mu/ Music: Mr. Ono Lennon says the internet has degraded the value of music in people's liv…
items.content_text: Mr. Ono Lennon says the internet has degraded the value of music in people's lives, since it's all widely available basically for free. He's right. Yes, it's convenient, but it comes at a cost. It's like how taking pictures has changed. You would only bring a camera to special occasions, for commemorating something or documenting milestones. In the 1800s, people used to need a photographer to do it. Now we all have cameras with us at all times, and it's great and useful, but photos undeniably ho

items.title: /g/ Technology: Cultural Technology
items.content_text: ITT we post pieces of technology that aren't universal, items that are unique to various cultures. The chukudu is a remarkable, hand-built wooden vehicle that serves as a vital economic lifeline in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, particularly around the city of Goma. Born out of necessity due to poor road infrastructure and the high cost of motorized transport, these robust, unmotorized scooters are ingeniously crafted from local wood and scrap metal, designed to carry massive load

items.title: /g/ Technology: AI facial recognition
items.content_text: Cops in my country are using ClearView AI to catch criminals. The AI has scraped social media for millions of pics of people's faces. How is that legal? Sure, I have an online presence. I put my photos online willingly. But I have never given consent for governments to steal my photos and use them for training material that is used in the legal system of all places. Actually, how is any AI scraping legal? Governments and companies have scraped 100% of the Internet and are using it to make Minori

items.content_text: Japanese magazines often releases polls about the most popular video game characters in Japan, but I never saw something similar in the West. items.title: /g/ Technology: >BPFDoor is a Linux based passive long-term backdoor used by China-based threat …
items.content_text: >BPFDoor is a Linux based passive long-term backdoor used by China-based threat actors. First seen in 2021, BPFDoor is named after its usage of Berkley Packet Filter (BPF) to execute single task instructions. BPFDoor supports multiple protocols for communicating with a C2 including TCP, UDP, and ICMP and can start local or reverse shells that bypass firewalls using iptables. https://attack.mitre.org/software/S 1161/ CommonSense™ me arse...

items.title: /mu/ Music: Songs with Epic Intros
items.content_text: I want to know if there are other songs like the following: "Foreplay/Longtime" by Boston "Funeral for a Friend/Love Lies" by Elton John "I Need a Lover" by John Mellencamp I distinctly like the following things about these: -feel like two separate pieces of music put together, but it works -the intro isnt single instruments and has the full band -no lyrics in the first half -very dynamic, almost prog writing in the intro half -catchy almost pop sensibilities in the 2nd half -prefer single track
